# Fix: `POST /api/user-mappings/bulk` returns 404 — Holistic Root Cause & Fix

## Context

The CLI `import-s3 --dry-run` command successfully authenticates and downloads from S3, but `POST /api/user-mappings/bulk` returns 404. The previous workaround added a CLI-side fallback (already implemented), but the **real root cause** is that the route is never registered by Micronaut — the backend log shows zero trace of the request (no security filter, no controller log), meaning the router rejects it before it enters the pipeline.

## Root Cause: Stale KSP Incremental Processing Cache

**Micronaut registers routes at compile time via KSP (Kotlin Symbol Processing), not at runtime.** When `@Post("/bulk")` was added to `UserMappingController.kt`, the incremental KSP processor did not regenerate the route definitions:

- `build.gradle.kts` has `incremental(true)` enabled for KSP
- `./gradlew build` reported `kspKotlin UP-TO-DATE` — KSP skipped reprocessing
- The method exists in compiled `.class` bytecode, but the KSP-generated `$UserMappingController$Definition.class` (which Micronaut reads for route registration) is stale and doesn't include `/bulk`
- Result: Micronaut's router has no entry for `POST /api/user-mappings/bulk` → returns 404 at the router level, before any filter or controller code executes → no log trace

This explains why the backend shows `GET /api/user-mappings` working (that route was in the original KSP output) but `POST /api/user-mappings/bulk` is invisible.

## Fix (3 parts)

### 1. Backend: Force KSP regeneration (fixes the root cause)

```bash
./gradlew clean build
```

This deletes `build/generated/ksp/` and `build/kspCaches/`, forcing full reprocessing. The `$UserMappingController$Definition.class` will be regenerated with the `/bulk` route included.

No code changes needed — the controller, DTO, service, and repository code is already correct.

### 2. Backend: Add route debug logging for future diagnosis
